content:
# Multiphase Flow

**Summary**: The simultaneous flow of multiple phases (oil, gas, and water) in a single production line.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1997-al-taweel|Al-Taweel, 1997]] developed a multiphase flow metering set-up to replace large conventional test traps.

- [[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992]] studied multiphase flow in the context of choke design for production wells.

- [[2009-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2009]] studied multiphase flow through chokes to determine optimal production rates.

## Scientific Context

- [[1997-al-taweel|Al-Taweel, 1997]] Common in oil and gas production, requiring specialized equipment to separate or measure each component.

- [[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992]] studied multiphase flow in the context of choke design for production wells.

- [[2009-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2009]] studied multiphase flow through chokes to determine optimal production rates.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1997-al-taweel|Al-Taweel, 1997]] measured using Coriolis meters and a flow conditioner.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The system was designed to measure the rates of each phase (gas, oil, and water) in a multiphase stream. ([[1997-al-taweel|Al-Taweel, 1997, p. 15]])


- Multiphase flow is inherent to the daily production process of gas or oil. ([[1992-al-towailib|Al-Towailib, 1992, p. 24]])

- Two-phase flow behavior is categorized into critical and sub-critical based on velocity relative to the speed of sound. ([[2009-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2009, p. 29]])
